hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of Canada Day, a celebration of diversity and self-expression, Hairspray's energetic musical numbers and uplifting message of acceptance make it a perfect fit.
hero.blurb: This vibrant musical comedy is full of catchy songs, impressive dance moves, and a talented cast, making it a joyous film experience that will leave you smiling. With its themes of inclusivity and acceptance, Hairspray is a great way to kick off a celebration of diversity.

released.title: Liv Tyler's Reich: The Dark Knight
released.reason: Released today in 2008, The Dark Knight is still widely regarded as one of the best superhero films of all time, thanks to its gripping storyline, memorable performances, and impressive action sequences.
released.blurb: Christopher Nolan's gritty take on the caped crusader is both a thought-provoking thriller and an action-packed blockbuster, with Heath Ledger's iconic portrayal of the Joker being a particular highlight. The Dark Knight is a must-see for fans of the genre.

birthday.title: Peyton Place
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Lana Turner, born July 1, 1921, starring in this classic drama that explores the secrets and scandals of a small town, showcasing her impressive acting range.
birthday.blurb: This engaging melodrama is full of twists and turns, with a talented cast bringing the complex characters to life. Lana Turner's performance is particularly notable, making Peyton Place a great choice for fans of classic Hollywood cinema.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: a famously bad movie with terrible reviews, I Know Who Killed Me is a cringeworthy thriller that fails to deliver on its intriguing premise.
awful.blurb: This mess of a film is full of plot holes, poor acting, and a nonsensical storyline, making it a laughable example of how not to make a thriller. With a plethora of negative reviews, I Know Who Killed Me is a great example of a film that's so bad it's good.
